studies.title: Health Economic Evaluation of  costs and benefits of a computer based program called "Deprexis" used for insured persons of the DAK (statutory health insurance) suffering from depression
studies.health_condition:
ICD-10-GM (translation):
F32.0 - Mild depressive episode
ICD-10-GM (translation):
F32.1 - Moderate depressive episode
Healthy volunteers:
No Entry
studies.brief_summary: The main aim of this study is to analyse weather persons suffering from depression and treated by a computer based program called "Deprexis" save costs for the Statutory Health Insurance. Furthermore the study tries to prove that the program reduces depression and that quality of life, social functioning, satisfaction improve also by the application of "Deprexis". No physical medication is applied. All willing persons that suffer from depression and would like to take part in the program (insured persons of the DAK (Statutory Health Insurance in Germany)) are divided into two groups by random. One group - the intervention group - uses the program for a period of twelve weeks. The other group - the control group has no access to "Deprexis" and will serve to control the results concerning the above mentioned outcome measures.
studies.intervention:
Arm 1:
The intervention group uses the program Deprexis for a period of 12 weeks. The Questionnaires applied measure:
Depression: PHQ-9, quality of life: SF12 and EQ-5D, Questionnaire working and adjustment scale: WSAS. Costs are supllied by the statutory health insurance DAK (except ambulant costs).  Acceptance with service of the DAK and the program Deprexis. 
Arm 2:
The control group does not get access to the program "Deprexis". The Questionnaires measure:
Depression: PHQ-9, quality of life:

classification.reason: The study involves random assignment of participants into intervention and control groups, which is a hallmark of an RCT. The design aims to compare outcomes between these groups, and the description suggests a prospective interventional approach. While publication type and allocation details are not specified, the randomization and controlled comparison strongly indicate an RCT.

studies.title: Efficacy of a depression-focused internet intervention in slot machine gamblers: A randomized controlled trial
studies.health_condition:
ICD-10-GM (translation):
F63.0 - Pathological gambling
ICD-10-GM (translation):
F32 - Depressive episode
Healthy volunteers:
No Entry
studies.brief_summary: The aim of the present study is to examine the acceptance and efficacy of an online-intervention for depression (“Deprexis”) in a sample of problematic and pathological slot-machine gamblers. We hypothesize that the intervention group shows a greater reduction in both depressive and gambling-related symptoms compared to a wait-list control group. Within the study, 140 people with gambling-related symptoms and mood problems are randomly assigned to two groups (treatment group or wait-list control group). To measure a change in symptoms, different self-assessment questionnaires are collected at two assessment points.
studies.intervention:
Arm 1:

studies.title: Evaluation of the effectiveness of an online-based support program for the treatment of depression (Novego®) - A randomized controlled trial
studies.health_condition:
ICD-10-GM (translation):
F32.0 - Mild depressive episode
ICD-10-GM (translation):
F32.1 - Moderate depressive episode
ICD-10-GM (translation):
F32.2 - Severe depressive episode without psychotic symptoms
ICD-10-GM (translation):
F32.8 - Other depressive episodes
ICD-10-GM (translation):
F32.9 - Depr
studies.brief_summary:
The aim of the study is to test the effectiveness of the 3-month online program Novego - "Depressionen bewältigen"® in terms of reducing depressive symptoms, improving quality of life, and increasing self-esteem. 
In the course of the study, 200 people with depression will be randomly assigned to two groups (intervention group and waiting control group). All participants will be interviewed before, during, and after using the program. Various self-rating questionnaires will be given to measure changes in health-related variables. 
Greater improvement in symptoms, quality of life, and self-esteem is expected in the intervention group compared to the wait-list control group after 12 weeks.
studies.intervention:
Arm 1:
Intervention group = receives access to the internet-based support program Novego - "Depressionen bewältigen"®
Arm 2:
Waiting control group = receives no additional treatment during the intervention period (only "care as usual"); receives access to the intervention after 12 weeks (after post-survey)
Endpoints

studies.title:
Implementation of a digital nurse to improve the use of digital health applications for older people with depressive disorders (DiGA4Aged) -
A randomized proof of concept study.
studies.health_condition:
ICD-10-GM (translation):
F32.0 - Mild depressive episode
ICD-10-GM (translation):
F33.0 - Recurrent depressive disorder, current episode mild
ICD-10-GM (translation):
F32.1 - Moderate depressive episode
ICD-10-GM (translation):
F33.1 - Recurrent depressive disorder, current episode moderate
Healthy
studies.brief_summary: DiGA4Aged aims to increase the usage rate of digital health applications (DiGA) in older patients with mild to moderate depression. It has already been shown that DiGAs can be an effective support in psychotherapy, especially as a bridging measure in cases of long waiting times. Nevertheless, older people use DiGAs less frequently than younger people. The aim of the study is to examine whether the usage rate of a registered app for depression treatment can be improved in older patients by providing support in the form of a "digital nurse". The support (intervention) is to be offered over a period of 8 weeks after prescription of the DiGA. After a further 8 weeks of follow-up, the utilization rate will be compared with a group of patients who did not receive support in the form of a digital nurse.
studies.intervention:
Arm 1:
Participants in the intervention group receive: 
a) The prescription of a depression-specific DiGA
b) A brief explanation of the DiGA using the manufacturer's information videos.
c) 8 weeks of support and "digital coaching" by a digital nurse (main intervention)
Regarding a) 
The DiGA list of the BfArM currently contains two DiGAs that are approved for the treatment of people with depressive disorders. While "Selfapys online course for depression" (Krämer et al. 2022) is approved for use
